Description: QML's strength is reactive bindings that auto-update
Don't: Imperative updates in JS for derived values

Guideline: Keep QML files small and focused
Do: Extract reusable elements into separate .qml files

Guideline: Use custom components via file naming
Don't: Register components manually when file naming works

Description: Build complex UI from simple composable pieces

Guideline: Use required properties for component APIs
Description: Enforce mandatory inputs at compile time
Code Bad: property int duration // caller may forget

Guideline: Use property aliases for child exposure
Description: Expose child properties to parent without duplication
Don't: Duplicate properties with manual onChanged sync
Code Bad: property string text; onTextChanged: label.text = text

Don't: Assign with = then expect binding to work
Code Good: states: [ State { PropertyChanges { target: rect; color: "red" } } ]

Don't: Flat list of dozens of properties
Code Good: QtObject { id: internal; property bool playing: false; property int position: 0 }

Do: default property alias for child items

Guideline: Use States and Transitions for UI states
Description: QML has built-in state machine for visual states
Do: State for visual state changes with Transitions
Don't: Imperative if/else chains for visual properties
Code Bad: if (playing) { color = green } else { color = grey }

Guideline: Avoid complex JS logic in QML
Description: Business logic belongs in C++ backend
Do: JS only for simple UI logic and glue
Don't: Complex algorithms or data processing in QML JS
Code Good: C++ model with Q_PROPERTY exposed to QML

Guideline: Expose C++ state via Q_PROPERTY with NOTIFY
Description: Reactive properties bridge C++ to QML
Do: Q_PROPERTY with NOTIFY signal for every bound property
Don't: Direct method calls for state that should be reactive
Code Good: Q_PROPERTY(int position READ position NOTIFY positionChanged)

Guideline: Use QML_ELEMENT macro for C++ types
Description: Modern registration of C++ types in QML
Anchors are more performant than manual x/y bindings
Don't: Manual x/y/width/height bindings for relative layout
Code Bad: x: parent.x + 8; y: parent.y + 8; width: parent.width - 16

Description: Layout types handle sizing and spacing
Anchors and Layout attached properties conflict
Do: Use anchors OR Layout properties per item

Guideline: Set implicit sizes for custom components
Description: Components should declare their natural size
Don't: Require parent to set explicit sizes
Repeater in Column for large lists

Do: Specify height on delegate root item
Don't: Let delegate calculate its own height

Description: Group list items with section headers

Don't: Manual selection property in each delegate
Code Bad: property int selectedIndex; // manual tracking in each delegate

Guideline: Prefer C++ models for large data
Description: QAbstractItemModel is much faster than JS arrays
Don't: ListModel or JS arrays for 100+ items
Code Bad: ListModel { id: logModel } // slow for 1000+ items

Don't: Custom painted buttons for standard interactions
Wrap controls in Rectangles or rebuild from scratch
Code Bad: Rectangle { color: "#22C55E"; MouseArea { } } // not a real Button

Description: Built-in modal system with proper focus handling
Do: Popup with modal: true and dim
Code Good: Popup { modal: true; dim: true; anchors.centerIn: Overlay.overlay }
Code Bad: Rectangle { z: 100; visible: showDialog } // no focus trapping

Guideline: Use StackView or SwipeView for navigation
Code Bad: Item { LogView { visible: currentTab === 0 } PanelView { visible: currentTab === 1 } }

Guideline: Use Action for shared command handling
Do: Action with shortcut and triggered handler
Code Good: Action { id: playAction; shortcut: "Space"; onTriggered: engine.play() }
Code Bad: Button { onClicked: engine.play() } Shortcut { sequence: "Space"; onActivated: engine.play() }

Do: Behavior on x/y/opacity/color for smooth transitions
Code Bad: SequentialAnimation { id: fadeAnim } // triggered manually for simple fade

Guideline: Avoid animation on rapidly updating values
Description: 100ms timer ticks should not trigger 150ms animations
Code Good: text: formatTime(remaining) // direct update, no Behavior
Code Bad: Behavior on text { /* animates every 100ms tick — jank */ }

Guideline: Use Canvas or QQuickPaintedItem for custom drawing
Description: Pie charts and meters need pixel-level control
Do: Canvas/QQuickPaintedItem for pie counter and audio meters
Don't: QML Rectangles/rotation hacks for circular graphics
Code Good: Canvas { onPaint: { var ctx = getContext("2d"); ctx.arc(...) } }
Code Bad: Rectangle { rotation: angle; /* fake pie slice */ }

Do: Read animation preference and disable pulse/glow

Description: QML bindings are optimized; JS functions are not
Code Bad: visible: isPlaying(model.status) // function call every eval

Description: Lazy-load views that are not always visible
Code Bad: SoundPanel { visible: currentTab === 2 } // created but hidden

Description: clip: true forces extra render pass
Do: clip: true only on scrollable views

Description: Object allocation in bindings causes GC pressure
Do: Pre-allocate objects or use simple types

Description: Cache visual tree as texture for blur/glow
Code Bad: Rectangle { layer.enabled: true } // on every item

Do: Accessible.name on every button and control

Don't: Default role for custom interactive items
Code Bad: MouseArea { } // no role, invisible to screen reader

Description: Keyboard users must navigate all controls

Do: FocusScope for each panel (log list / sound panel / controls)
Don't: Flat focus chain across entire window
Code Good: FocusScope { id: transportControls; /* tab within group */ }
Code Bad: // all buttons in one flat tab chain

Description: Keyboard users need to see focus
Do: Rectangle border or highlight on activeFocus

Description: Clean data interface between C++ model and QML delegate

Don't: dataChanged without roles (triggers full delegate rebind)
Code Bad: emit dataChanged(idx, idx) // rebinds all roles
m_items.append(item); emit layoutChanged(); // resets entire view
// JS filter in onModelChanged: for(var i...) { if(...) }

Description: QML for UI declaration; C++ for business logic and performance
Do: C++ for audio engine / log engine / timers / models
Don't: Complex business logic in QML JS
Code Good: // C++: LogPlayEngine exposes Q_PROPERTY / signals; QML: binds to them
Code Bad: // QML: function playNext() { /* 50 lines of JS logic */ }

Don't: Passing context objects through every component
Code Good: // C++: QML_SINGLETON class AppConfig; QML: AppConfig.stationName
Code Bad: // QML: property var config passed through 8 levels

Description: React to backend events in QML
Code Bad: Timer { interval: 100; repeat: true; onTriggered: checkState() }

Don't: Manual qmldir files and resource management
Code Good: qt_add_qml_module(airplay URI Airplay VERSION 1.0 QML_FILES Main.qml)

Guideline: Use Qt Quick Test for QML
Code Bad: // only C++ unit tests, no QML coverage

Code Good: // QTest: verify rowCount, data(role), insert/remove signals
Code Bad: // only manual testing via running the app
